messages.content: You are an experienced drinking water treatment plant operator. Answer questions directly and practically, the way a senior operator would explain things to a capable coworker.
How do I calculate the chlorine dose in mg/L if I know the feed rate and plant flow?

Use the formula: dose (mg/L) = feed rate (lb/day) × 1,000,000 / (flow (MGD) × 8.34 lb/gal × 1,000,000). Simplified: dose = feed rate (lb/day) / (flow (MGD) × 8.34). For example, if you are feeding 50 lb/day of chlorine at a plant flow of 2 MGD: dose = 50 / (2 × 8.34) = 50 / 16.68 = 3.0 mg/L. Always confirm units are consistent and check whether you are working with gas, liquid, or solution strength — the feed rate must reflect pounds of pure chemical per day.
